title: Toasted Mexican Tortilla Churros
description: A crunchy Mexican dish featuring flour tortilla, served in a stone bowl, jain friendly and in the form of churros. It is a cultured meal prep dish from the Yucatecan region.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the flour tortilla into long strips, about 1 inch wide and 4 inches long.
In a bowl, mix together the granulated sugar, cinnamon powder, and salt.
Dip each tortilla strip into the sugar mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Fry the coated tortilla strips in the hot oil until golden brown and crispy.
Drain the fried churros on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Dust the churros with powdered sugar and serve warm.
If desired, melt the chocolate chips in a microwave safe bowl and dip the churros into the melted chocolate.
Serve the churros in a stone bowl and enjoy.
